item.title: Toby Fox Shares New Development Update On Deltarune Chapter 6
item.content:encoded:
Work on Chapter 7 may also start this year

Following the announcement Deltarune Chapter 5 will be coming to the Switch and Switch 2 on 24th June 2026, Toby Fox has shared an update about Deltarune Chapter 6.

In the latest newsletter, it's mentioned how this particular chapter is "developing well" and that it's actually been easier to make than others. In fact, it's going so well, development on Chapter 7 might be underway before the end of 2026.

Here's the update and what exactly the team has been working on in Chapter 6:

Chapter 6 Progress Update

Chapter 6 is developing well.

  • Cutscenes / NPC interactions: Making good progress.
  • Overworld gameplay: We’ve created all the basic gimmicks of the chapter. (Gimmick = climbing, puzzles, etc.) Now, we need to create a few more rooms utilizing these gimmicks.
  • Normal enemies & bullets: Mostly complete.
  • Boss & bullets: We are working on the bullets of the last battle of the chapter.

This Chapter is easier to make than the others, so the development is going quite fast.

This sounds crazy, but it’s not unrealistic that some staff members may start working on Chapter 7 before the end of the year.

When more is shared about Deltarune Chapter 6 and 7, we'll be sure to provide an update. For now, the focus is on the fifth chapter, arriving later this month. You can see what it's about in the trailer in our previous post:

Will you be playing Deltarune 5? Have you played the previous chapters yet? Let us know in the comments.

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply

item.title: 9 R.I.P. sequel launches this fall in the west
item.content:encoded:
• Switch

Official English website updated.

Otome visual novel sequel 9 R.I.P. sequel will launch for Switch this fall in the west, publisher Idea Factory International announced. It will be available both physically and digitally. A limited edition will also be available.

9 R.I.P sequel first launched for Switch on September 25, 2025 in Japan.

The official English website has also been updated:

  • The Character page now features descriptions and quotes from both beloved returning characters and new side characters central to the Love Interests’ respective routes.
  • System pages have also been updated and reveal more information regarding new choice types such as Purity and Depth choices within routes.
  • Sound page has been updated with theme song information.

Here is an overview of the game, via Idea Factory International:

About

Following the endings of 9 R.I.P., Misa Isshiki finds herself at a crossroads with navigating romantic life and the consequences of her actions during her second year of high school. New challenges await depending on the route taken…

Whether it’s grappling with the fallout of her death, or navigating a relationship with a supernatural being, Misa must come to terms with her decisions to move forward.

Will she continue along the same path, or take another route entirely?

Key Features

  • Back from the Dead – 9 R.I.P. has returned from the grave with a sequel! With 9 full routes of love interests from the previous game, there’s sure to be a man, demon, or spirit for everyone’s tastes.
  • We’re in Heaven – Heavenly Routes are back with a more direct sequel to the previous Heavenly Endings. These stories feature new obstacles to overcome. Make your choices carefully, or they will have consequences for everyone around you… Can you and your love prevail through the challenges of life (and possibly death)?
  • Sugar, Spice, and Everything Nice – Need something more on the romantic side to sweep you off your feet? Godly Routes suit fans who crave a happy and sweet ending, serving as epilogues for each Love Interest’s Godly Ending in the first game.
  • Killer Views – Character designer Yuuya kills with breathtaking visuals that once again manage to bring even the dead back to life.


https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/9-rip-sequel-launches-this-fall-in-the-west

item.title: Tamagotchi Plaza ‘Resort’ update now available
item.content:encoded:
• Switch
  • Switch 2

Create a cozy place where Tamagotchi can rest.

The “Resort” update for Tamagotchi Plaza is now available, publisher Bandai Namco and developer HYDE announced.

Here is an overview of the update, via Bandai Namco:

In this update, Prince Tamahiko creates Maison de Poka, a “Resort” where hardworking shop managers of Tamahiko Town can relax and recharge. As the manager of the Resort, players can design cozy rooms for the ten hardworking shop managers.

Players will be gifted with furniture upon their first visit to the Resort and can jump straight into decorating their room. Additional furniture can also be purchased at Pariparitchi’s furniture store, “Paripari Style,” which features a variety of themed furniture, with new selections changing daily.

Players will be able to create endless possibilities by collecting pieces from their favorite theme or mixing and matching freely. While decorating, players can watch visiting Tamagotchi relax and reveal an off-duty side not seen in the shops. Players might even overhear chats and thoughts as the characters unwind.

Discovering which Tamagotchi will drop by is all part of the fun, and as players collect more furniture and continue decorating, they can upgrade the Resort to design up to two rooms.

Tamagotchi Plaza is available now for Switch 2 and Switch.

Watch a new trailer below.

“Resort” Update Trailer

English

Japanese

item.title: Deltarune Chapter 5 Release Date Revealed as Toby Fox Offers Promising Update for the Future
item.content:encoded:
Deltarune Chapter 5 finally has a release date, and Undertale mastermind Toby Fox has used the opportunity to give fans a progress update on Chapter 6.

The yearlong wait for more from the lead developer's parallel story RPG finally came to an end when it showed up for today's June 2026 Nintendo Direct. The reveal came with a release date of June 24, 2026, promising to launch the fifth major update for PC, Nintendo Switch, Switch 2, PlayStation 4, and PlayStation 5 in just 15 days.

It's been a little more than one year since Chapter 4 launched, so Fox made sure to make the wait worth it with a 46-second video that showed just enough while saving some of the best moments for full release. You can see the teaser footage in the Deltarune Chapter 5 trailer below.

Immediately following Deltarune's Nintendo Direct cameo, Fox took to his newsletter to share a little bit more behind-the-scenes info. After a quick apology for the wait, he explained the trailer itself was made by artist Everdraed, with its music performed and arranged by Carlos "insaneintherain" Eiene. Animator, developer, and Undertale's Temmie, Temmie Chang, handled the lovely art featured at the end of the trailer.

"In Chapter 4, we saw dark clouds on the horizon… That’s why, let’s not look there for now," Fox said when describing Deltarune Chapter 5. "Let’s turn around and watch the sun, before it goes down completely. Let’s smile again. Let’s have one more fun adventure, okay?"

"In my opinion, it fits well in the overall story of the game," he continued.

With the next chapter just weeks away, Fox is ready to offer a progress update on Chapter 6. He said it's "developing well," explaining there's been good progress on things like cutscenes and NPC interactions. Fox even went as far as to say that "this Chapter is easier to make than the others, so the development is going quite fast."

Deltarune Chapter 5 Release Date Trailer Nintendo Direct June 2026 - Screenshots

"This sounds crazy, but it’s not unrealistic that some staff members may start working on Chapter 7 before the end of the year," Fox added.

As Deltarune Chapter 5 prepares to launch and work on Chapter 6 progresses smoothly, the wait for the seventh and final chapter may not be quite as long as some believed. In the meantime, you can read IGN's 9/10 review of its first few chapters. You can also catch up on everything else announced at the June 2026 Nintendo Direct.

Michael Cripe is a freelance writer with IGN. He's best known for his work at sites like The Pitch, The Escapist, and OnlySP. Be sure to give him a follow on Bluesky (@mikecripe.bsky.social) and Twitter (@MikeCripe).

Recommends

item.title: Jujutsu Kaisen Rumble: Survivaton Revealed as Unexpected Collab From the Vampire Survivors Team
item.content:encoded:
Jujutsu Kaisen Rumble: Survivaton Revealed as Unexpected Collab From the Vampire Survivors Team

The world feels so, so wonderful right now.

Vampire Survivors developer Poncle is mixing its laidback shoot-'em-up gameplay with one of the most popular anime out there for a new game called Jujutsu Kaisen Rumble: Survivaton.

The unexpected collaboration made its debut at today's Nintendo Direct. With the original Vampire Survivors and this year's Vampire Crawlers now under its belt, Poncle is looking to Yuji Itadori to help fight off a never-ending army of cursed spirits.

The first trailer for Jujutsu Kaisen Rumble: Survivaton should absolutely look familiar to fans of the studio's previous work. Instead of classic ghouls and ghosts, it gets a Jujutsu Kaisen facelift while still retaining that pixelated style and one-more-run gameplay that helped Vampire Survivors become so popular. It's a multiplayer anime spin on a tried-and-true formula, and you can see its Nintendo Direct reveal trailer below.

"Players battle wave after wave of cursed spirits while competing against up to seven rivals in fast-paced online multiplayer matches," an official description for Jujutsu Kaisen Rumble: Survivaton said. "Choose from 20+ playable characters from the Jujutsu Kaisen universe and use simple movement-based controls with automatic attacks, where success depends on strategic positioning and timing. Players earn points by defeating enemies, unlock game-changing 'Rule Additions' every 100 points, and compete for the top spot in dynamic rankings.

"The experience culminates in a final duel between the top two players. JUJUTSU KAISEN RUMBLE: SURVIVATON readies for battle on Nintendo Switch 2 in 2026."

With more than 20 playable characters from across the Jujutsu Kaisen universe to play as, we can almost certainly expect to see loads of fan-favorite names. The trailer teased more than a few, too, including Yuji, Megumi Fushiguro, Nobara Kugisaki, Toge Inumaki, Kento Nanami, Yuta Okkotsu, and, of course, Satoru Gojo.

Jujutsu Kaisen Rumble: Survivaton

TBD on how robust the single-player offerings in Jujutsu Kaisen Rumble: Survivaton are, but its trailer at least confirmed that there is an offline mode with computer-controlled opponents. Its price and release date have not been revealed outside of Poncle's promise to launch before the year is out.

In addition to Nintendo Switch 2, Jujutsu Kaisen Rumble: Survivaton is set to come to PC via Steam, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X|S. Whenever it does release, it should help with the wait for The Culling Game Part 2 after the Jujutsu Kaisen anime left fans waiting for more with the end of Season 3 earlier this year. For all of the biggest reveals shown during today's showcase, you can see everything announced at the Nintendo Direct for June 2026 right here.

Michael Cripe is a freelance writer with IGN. He's best known for his work at sites like The Pitch, The Escapist, and OnlySP. Be sure to give him a follow on Bluesky (@mikecripe.bsky.social) and Twitter (@MikeCripe).

item.title: Destiny 2 Hits Highest Player Count in Two Years Following Final Update's Release, Shatters Marathon's All-Time Peak
item.content:encoded:
Destiny 2 is seeing a massive resurgence with the release of its final update.

Destiny fans have shown up in droves to send off the beloved sci-fi shooter. Although Destiny 2's servers will remain active for the foreseeable future, the game won't be getting any new content going forward. The game's final update was released today and at the time of writing, the game's 24-hour concurrent player count on Steam has peaked at 167,000. This is the highest Destiny 2 player count since the release of 2024's The Final Shape, the last paid expansion for the game, which peaked at 314,000 players.

Some players have even been reporting that they're having troubles logging into Destiny 2 due to the sudden influx. Ahead of the update, fans were aiming to surpass the all-time peak of Marathon and crash Destiny 2's servers to show Bungie/Sony that Destiny should continue on. They've already blown past Marathon's all-time peak player count of 77,000 players, and it seems the campaign has been successful in creating instability in Destiny 2's servers. Given this has all happned in the middle of the workday in the United States, it'll be interesting to see if this rockets further in the evening or closer to the weekend.

Destiny 2: The Final Shape Screenshots

Last month, Bungie announced that support from Destiny 2 would be coming to an end in June, which caught fans off guard as many were expecting more expansions and updates. However, Bungie has chosen to focus its efforts on supporting Marathon, its latest release which has failed to attract a significant amount of regular players and has resulted in substantial losses for Sony, Bungie's parent company.

Destiny fans have been incredibly upset since the news broke, even going as far to demand a third game during Summer Game Fest. Unfortunately, Destiny 3 doesn't appear to be in the cards as Bloomberg reports that a new game isn't in active development. Additionally, Bungie is planning "significant" layoffs, although the company is said to be pitching and prototyping new games in the Destiny universe.

The cost to make such a game is likely too high given Bungie's financial woes and it wouldn't release until the early 2030s. But stranger things have happened, so maybe Destiny 3 could happen one day. It just won't be anytime soon.

Cade Onder is a freelancer for IGN's news team. He covers all things entertainment, including gaming, film, and more. You can find him on Twitter @Cade_Onder.

Recommends

item.title: Everbloom adds Switch 2 version, launches in spring 2027
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • Switch 2

Published by Fictions.

Everbloom, the Torbie-developed cozy farming simulation game announced in 2024, will launch for Switch 2 and PC via Steam and Epic Games Store in spring 2027, newly revealed publisher Fictions announced.

“We’re incredibly excited to finally share more of Everbloom with players,” said Torbie co-founder and CEO Zabir Hoque in a press release. “The team has spent the last couple of years refining the experience and building a world that feels comforting, playful and full of whimsical moments.”

Fictions producer Kelsey Hansen added, “When we first met with Zabir, Riley, Sheridan and the team at Torbie Games, we felt immediately there was a real sense of magic to Everbloom. It’s a place filled with warmth, character and creativity, and we’re so excited for players to make it their own in 2027.”

Here is an overview of the game, via Torbie:

About

Everbloom is a cute and cozy farming social sim set in a world of whimsy—grow buckets of popcorn right on the plant and make chocolate milk by feeding your cows candy bars. Grow, care, build, decorate, and explore the world of Everbloom solo or with your friends.

Build your Homestead

Dream big, create a home. Transform your farm into whatever you desire. Design, craft, and customize your own homestead from the ground up. Whether you’re constructing buildings, laying out gardens, or designing cozy corners, your homestead reflects your personal style and creativity. In Everbloom, using your imagination can unlock whimsical results. Sometimes you need to set your peppers on fire to make them spicy.

Tend and Befriend

From the ordinary to the extraordinary, nurture a diverse menagerie. Each animal has its own personality that can be passed down between parents and child. Explore your world to find wild animals with unique traits to collect. Can you raise the perfect Strawberry Cow?

One to Four-Player Cooperative Play

Bring your friends into your world with cooperative multiplayer. Work together to farm, build, decorate, and explore. Share resources, collaborate on projects, and enjoy community events together in a truly interconnected experience.

Explore Your World

Explore a handcrafted intimate open world, where every path leads to a new discovery. From a bustling town to lush forests, hidden groves, and deep mysterious caves—discover each area’s secrets and stories.

Become a Part of a Community

In the world of Everbloom, flavorful foods have been lost to time. New in town, you must track down and rediscover the lost heirlooms to bring flavor back into the world. Engage with a cast of colorful characters who bring the world of Everbloom to life. Forge friendships, help your neighbors, and ask townsfolk on dates to become an integral part of a community that grows and thrives with your participation.

Key Features

  • Craft and Build – Construct everything from furniture, buildings, equipment, and tools.
  • Fishing – Explore the world and catch a variety of fish.
  • Cooking – Mix and match ingredients until you get the perfect result.
  • Flower Breeding – Blend seed colors to grow unique flower colors.
  • Townsfolk – Befriend the townsfolk who breathe life into your adventure. Each character offers unique stories and secrets.
  • Romance – Go on dates and forge connections that could last a lifetime.
  • Customization – Choose from various animal types, colors, and clothing options to reflect your personal style.

Watch a new trailer bleow.

Release Window Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/everbloom-adds-switch-2-version-launches-in-spring-2027

item.title: Rise of the Tomb Raider: 20 Year Celebration now available for Switch 2
item.content:encoded:
• Switch 2

Gripping exploration, storytelling, and action.

Aspyr and Crystal Dynamics have released a Switch 2 version of Rise of the Tomb Raider: 20 Year Celebration via Nintendo eShop. It is $26.99 with a 10 percent-off launch discount until June 23, after which the price will change to $29.99.

Rise of the Tomb Raider first launched for Xbox One and Xbox 360 on November 10, 2015, followed by PC on January 82, 2016, and PlayStation 4 on October 11, 2016.

Here is an overview of the Switch 2 version, via Aspyr:

No turning back… Discover the secrets of Kitezh and survive the forces of Trinity on Lara’s first tomb raiding expedition!

Our fierce explorer is back to encounter the unknown. Embark on Lara’s most treacherous journey through Siberia to the lost city of Kitezh and unlock the mysteries of the past.

  • An Exhilarating Story – Outwit the insidious secret society, Trinity, and their paramilitary operatives as you race to uncover the Divine Source, said to harness the power of immortality, hidden deep in the Siberian wilderness.
  • Challenging Puzzle Tombs – Follow in the footsteps of those before you—the legendary Prophet, the ancient Remnant civilization, and Trinity—to outwit environmental puzzles and ancient traps guarding the relics within.
  • Dynamic Combat – Prove you’re worthy of the Croft legacy as you outmaneuver enemies using scavenged supplies and Lara’s versatile arsenal. Engage in combat or utilize stealth to silently eliminate Trinity soldiers.
  • Survival Upgrades and Customization – Gather resources to craft essential gear and upgrade Lara’s skills. Discover weapon parts and ancient texts that will help you create the specialized ammunition Lara needs to survive against the relentless forces of nature and of Trinity.

The expedition continues! All downloadable content and exclusive content are included in this complete edition:

  • All Season Pass Content and Downloadable Content – “Baba Yaga: The Temple of the Witch,” “Cold Darkness Awakened,” “Blood Ties,” “Lara’s Nightmare,” “Endurance Mode,” and “Co-Op Endurance.”
  • All Exclusives – Eight downloadable content outfits and seven downloadable content weapons.
    All Extras  35 expansion cards, “Extreme Survivor” difficulty, five classic Lara skins, and the Tomb Raider III reimagined “Antarctica Outfit.”

Watch the launch trailer below.

Switch 2 Launch Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/rise-of-the-tomb-raider-20-year-celebration-now-available-for-switch-2
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Observer: System Redux coming to Switch 2 on June 18
item.content:encoded:
• Switch 2

The first‑person cyberpunk horror detective game.

Bloober Team will release a Switch 2 version of first‑person cyberpunk horror detective game Observer: System Redux on June 18, the developer announced. Pre-orders are available now via Nintendo eShop at a 25 percent-off discount price of $29.99. The price will change to $39.99 after July 7.

Observer: System Redux first launched for Xbox Series and PC via Steam, Epic Games Store, and GOG on November 10, 2020, followed by PlayStation 5 on November 12, 2020, and PlayStation 4 and Xbox One on July 16, 2021.

Here is an overview of the game, via Bloober Team:

About

A first‑person cyberpunk horror detective story set in a decaying, neon‑scarred future.

The year is 2084. The future has turned out much darker than anyone could imagine. First, there was the Nanophage. A digital plague that killed thousands upon thousands of those who chose to augment their minds and bodies. Then came the War, leaving both the West and the East decimated and shattered. With no one left to seize power, corporations took over and forged their own crooked empires.

You are an Observer—a tool of corporate oppression. Feared and despised, you hack into the darkest corners of your suspects’ minds. You creep into their dreams, expose their fears, and extract whatever your investigation may require.

In this science-fiction tale crafted by Bloober Team—the modern‑day masters of horror, prepare to experience a dystopian future where not even your innermost thoughts are safe.

Key Features

  • Become a Neural Detective – You play as Daniel Lazarski, an elite investigator of the future portrayed by late cyberpunk icon Rutger Hauer. As an Observer, you hack into the minds of suspects to extract clues and evidence. Anything they felt, thought, or remembered can and will be used to solve your case.
  • Eat Their Dreams – Using a device known as the Dream Eater, hack into the minds of the dead and dying to relive their final moments to explore their fears and obsessions. Delve into these twisted neural mazes and search for clues that can help you find the elusive killer.
  • Take in the Atmosphere of 2084 – Enter a world ravaged by war and cyber-plague, where life is cheap and hope is scarce. Where most people will do anything to escape their grim existence. Virtual realities, mind-altering drugs, neural implants – pick your poison and drift away into a blissful oblivion. But remember, no matter how deep you go, you can never hide from an Observer.

Watch a new trailer below.

Switch 2 Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/observer-system-redux-coming-to-switch-2-on-june-18

item.title: Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine II coming to Switch 2 this holiday
item.content:encoded:
• Switch 2

Currently available for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC.

Publisher Focus Entertainment and developer Saber Interactive will release a Switch 2 version of third-person action game Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine II this holiday, the companies announced.

Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine II first launched for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam and Epic Games Store on September 9, 2024.

Here is an overview of the game, via Saber Interactive:

Enjoy intense, gory, fast-paced third-person action with hundreds of enemies on screen when Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine II releases on Nintendo Switch 2 this holiday season. Fight the enemies of Mankind as Lieutenant Demetrian Titus in solo or three-player cooperative play in this sequel to 2011’s Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine, or jump in endlessly brutal and replayable player-versus-environment and player-versus-player modes as your own Space Marine!

Customize the perks, weapons, and appearance of seven playable classes and join more than 12 million players to enjoy over two years of post-launch support, featuring new missions, game modes, and challenges to complete. For the Emperor!

item.title: Tavern Talk Stories: Dreamwalker Review - Serving Up New Stories On The Rocks
item.content:encoded:
Tavern Talk Stories Dreamwalker is a standalone visual novel experience from developer Gentle Troll Entertainment, where the stories and events are set in the overarching Tavern Talk world. According to the developer, Dreamwalker is set "36 years before the events" of the first game, where players get to enjoy a relaxing gameplay experience with new and familiar characters.

Akin to the first game, you get to play as the owner and barkeeper of a newly refurbished tavern where you get to dazzle the crowds with your superb mixology skills and attentive listening to guide them in their personal journeys. At the same time, another world-ending catastrophe requires your assistance as you sort out rumors and embark on quests to learn the truth behind this event and prevent these nightmares from becoming a reality.

Starting A New Tab At The Drowsy Dragon

Set multiple years before the original game, a new adventure awaits us as we return to the magical realm of Asteria, this time visiting the coastal region where a new story is ready to be explored. A new tavern is at the center of this new tale where new and familiar faces converge, new quests and branching paths unfold, new recipes and tonics to appease (or disappoint) the clientele, and ultimately change the "course of fate" for many individuals.

As the owner of The Drowsy Dragon tavern, situated on the picturesque Borkam coast, you get to showcase your barkeeping and social skills with your customers and establish new friendly connections with them throughout the main story. This is done by utilizing your magical gifts of concocting and serving unique yet seasonal beverages that provide much-needed buffs, inspiration, or motivation while listening to stories of hope, sadness, and adventures from those who step inside the tavern.

However, these delightful drinks not only grant unique buffs or provide a source of encouragement, inspiration, or motivation but can also change the fate of your customers. Nevertheless, as you're listening to these thrilling tales, your ears will pick up on some intriguing rumors, which you can sort similar rumors together to form unique quests that your fellow companions can accept from the commissions board.

Rumor Has It That Something Strange Is Brewing

Heavily inspired by Dungeons & Dragons (D&D), Dreamwalker is a visual novel game, first and foremost, and as such, it primarily focuses on branching narratives and thoughtful yet immersive reading throughout your playthrough. Reading text is one of the core gameplay mechanics, as clicking on text prompts provided and their subsequent dialogue options advances not just the game progression but the overall story.

The game does provide players with valuable Quality of Life features like the option to speed through these heavy dialogue sections and another feature that allows you access to past dialogue interactions. This backlog feature lets players view not only all dialogue interactions to decipher important information, but also clues that can help them when mixing drinks.

Although it will take you a few playthroughs to explore every character and to unlock all three endings, every branching path will be presented to you, the player, in a meaningful way, whether it be dialogue options or action prompts. These options or prompts, along with the drinks mixed and served to your customers, can change their fates and, as such, change future dialogue options presented to you in forthcoming interactions.

The rumors system got reworked and paired with the quest system, where you get to sort and group similar clues and rumors, and once all the correct clues or rumors have been compiled, they'll form a quest. This becomes available via the commissions board inside the tavern, which your fellow companions will accept throughout the playthrough, and completing these quests helps advance the story and influence future character interactions.

Alongside the quest-building mechanic, players can also experiment with mixology, where they can make use of various tonics available and those they'll acquire throughout the playthrough. Likewise, they'll obtain various recipes and combine various tonics to ensure the drinks not only have the right stats to be buffed but also based on clues provided from their companions.

Like with other visual novel games with drink mixing features, concoct the wrong drink for your companion, and as mentioned before, this could change not only future dialogue options but also their fate. Luckily, for players, they don't have to concern themselves with tavern management like in Tavern Keeper or any in-game economy, as none of these features are present in the game, so kick back, relax, and enjoy mixing wondrous drinks and the company of your friends.

It's All One Continuous Loop

Dreamwalker has borrowed some features from the original game, but it also reintroduces refreshed mechanics for an immersive experience to accompany the new stories. This gameplay loop is very easy to understand, especially for new players: you start each chapter by welcoming your companions to the tavern, listening to their thrilling tales, drink mixing, and collecting a rumor or two to make a quest and "rinse and repeat" before the next chapter begins.

This gameplay loop can get quite repetitive for some players after some time, especially during chapters that are dialogue-heavy and contain little to no quest-building or drink-mixing minigames to break up the slow pacing. These drink-mixing minigames are what make these loops enjoyable, as the process of combining different tonics or elixirs to get the correct attributes makes every minigame unique and different.

Overall, the gameplay systems and features that are present in Dreamwalker are nothing too overwhelming or painstakingly arduous to complete, and are simple enough for newcomers to understand. However, simplification can become quite tedious as it causes a loss of challenge that typically would entice our minds, which is the case with the game's general gameplay loop.

There wasn't anything present that really ramped up the challenge for our brains in an engaging manner, instead feeling quite disconnected from the story. There were instances of this experienced, and recapping with past dialogue became an additional task to complete, making the entire experience quite tedious even after several hours of gameplay.

Tavern Talk Stories Dreamwalker Review | Final Thoughts

Dreamwalker serves as the perfect entry to this Tavern Talk series, and overall, a wonderful game to be introduced to and enjoy for players unfamiliar with the visual novel genre. The game doesn't demand much of your time, unless you're aiming for a completionist run, as you can enjoy spending time in the tavern, mixing and serving up drinks, and listening to stories one chapter at a time.

The story is new and intriguing, set many years before the original game, with new and familiar characters visiting your tavern to share their adventures or whatever torments their mind and heart. The developers have reworked a few gameplay mechanics and features borrowed from the original Tavern Talk game, including the drink and rumors systems, for a more streamlined experience.

The gameplay loop is where some players may experience some conflicted emotions, as it can be a thrilling encounter in one chapter or it can feel monotonous at times. Aside from listening to stories, mixing drinks, and sorting rumors, there isn't much else to get overwhelmingly excited for, and that's not a bad thing, as there are other great things to enjoy, such as the interactions with characters and the memories and stories shared.

Tavern Talk Stories: Dreamwalker was reviewed on PC via Steam, with a copy provided by the publisher over the course of 16 hours - all screenshots were taken during the review.

Ashleigh is a freelance writer based in Cape Town, South Africa, who has previously contributed to GINX TV and various South African-based publications,… More about Ashleigh



https://techraptor.net/gaming/reviews/tavern-talk-stories-dreamwalker-review-serving-up-new-stories-on-rocks

item.title: In Am I Nima, you must find and fuse words to convince your mother you're really her daughter
item.content:encoded:
The upcoming visual novel just received a release date

Oh dear, here's an existentially uncomfortable (and genuinely potentially triggering around themes of abuse) one for you. Am I Nima, shown off this weekend during the Story Rich Showcase, is a visual novel in which you take on the role of the titular Nima, who is trying to convince her mother that she really is her daughter by combining words in her brain.

Shall we unpack that a touch? Nima has been trapped in the basement by her mother, with said parental figure testing her in order to discover the legitimacy of whether Nima is who she says she is. Throughout the game, Nima's brain can be seen to one side, devoid of any kind of thought. Over time, various words may appear that you can then use to respond to questions asked by Nima's mother, these questions and conversations in turn summoning new words. You can also search Nima's house to find new words too, something that's quite key outside of conversations.

The key thing here is in the fusion of words to create new ones. For example, Nima's mother might show her a plushie of a shark. You might already have the word bed from seeing your own bed, the word dad from a drawing on your wardrobe, and the word shark from a conversation. Combining the three will then give you the words aquarium and happy, allowing you to respond in kind to Nima's mother's questions.

It's a really unique looking approach to visual novel dialogue trees, blending well with the sensitive nature of the story. Your main goal is to figure out why Nima has been locked in the basement, and what else she might be hiding, so again, this one does seem like it might be quite a difficult experience. If it is something you'd be interested in, it just received a release date of October 8th too, and you can wishlist it on Steam (and try out a demo) right now.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/in-am-i-nima-you-must-find-and-fuse-words-to-convince-your-mother-youre-really-her-daughter
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: I Wasn’t Expecting Dragon’s Dogma 2 DLC In The Nintendo Direct
item.content:encoded:
I don’t know anyone who had “Dragon’s Dogma 2 gets a whole expansion” on their Nintendo Direct bingo cards today. The game is over two years old, and Capcom hasn’t said or done anything to suggest it would be giving Dragon’s Dogma 2 the same loving treatment it did to the original Dragon’s Dogma with its Dark Arisen expansion. But it is, and it’s even also titling this new expansion “Dark Arisen” as either a joke, a nod, or a suggestion that this is going to be somewhat similar to the widely praised first game’s Dark Arisen expansion.

I loved Dragon’s Dogma 2 a lot. I’m stoked this is happening. But after poking my nose into my Dragon’s Dogma 2 save file just now, I’m a little worried it’s going to be too much work for me to actually play it.

For those who are unfamiliar or who, like me, have forgotten over the last two years: Dragon’s Dogma 2 only allows players to keep a single save file. When you load up the game, you can continue either from your last save, or from the last inn that you slept at. But that’s it. There’s no saving the game and then rolling back decisions you made if you screw up. That is admittedly half the joy of this game: Dragon’s Dogma 2 has some gnarly stuff that can happen, right down to horrible city-destroying diseases and character death, and it really ruins the effects of it all if you can just save-scum your way out of it.

But there’s a problem that arises when you add DLC into the equation. Now, we don’t know much about the new Dark Arisen DLC just yet. According to social posts from the official account, it will add a new region to the game complete with new characters, dungeons, weapons, and skills. That’s cool! I want to do that! We don’t yet know where or how the new region will be accessed, or if you’ll need to meet certain level or story requirements to get into it. In most games, this isn’t a big deal for folks like me who played the game a year ago, as we’d just boot up our near-the-end-of-the-game save and waltz right in.

#DD2DA Overview Part 1 – New Stories in a New Area

Dragon's Dogma 2: Dark Arisen introduces a new area in the north, the snowy, long-abandoned Norgan region!

Set forth in this Forsaken Dominion and join forces with the enigmatic Eir, who seeks to unravel the secrets of the… pic.twitter.com/2Hx5bhTqzW

— Dragon's Dogma (@DragonsDogma) June 9, 2026

But in Dragon’s Dogma 2, things are a bit different. I don’t want to get into specifics for fear of spoilers, but there’s a very, very significant point of no return at the tail end of Dragon’s Dogma 2 that, if taken, will leave you stranded in a situation that is…not exactly conducive to exploring a new continent. And there’s no way back from it. The only way to get back to the original state of the world is to start a brand new game from the beginning. With only one save file, if you went past that point of no return (and you would have had to, to get to the game’s true ending), you’re locked in.

There are a lot of ways Capcom could deal with this, and to its credit, it seems like it’s setting things up to make things as easy as possible for returning players. New Game+ brings you back at the same level you ended the game on, with access to high-level pawns and all the vocation levels and abilities you had before. You could just start a New Game+ and beeline it to wherever you’re going. But even if you do that, it’s still a pretty time-consuming process to get back into things. Dragon’s Dogma 2 has no fast travel outside of portcrystals which require the use of ferrystones, a pretty scant consumable resource, and those don’t carry over into your new save. So you’ll still have to run all over the map and probably knock out at least a few quests you’ve already done before to even get to the new content. That’s probably not a big deal for die-hard fans playing the game many times over, but if you’re a one-and-done gamer like me, it’s irritating.

Updates to Dragon's Dogma 2 will be rolling out over the coming months on PS5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC!

In the lead-up to Dragon's Dogma 2: Dark Arisen, we'll be releasing two major Title Updates to improve and enhance the base game. First up is TU3.1 on June 10, which brings a… pic.twitter.com/ndl1TBl3oK

— Dragon's Dogma (@DragonsDogma) June 9, 2026

I’m pleased to see that in the lead-up to this release, Capcom is adding a bunch of quality-of-life improvements, including improvements to fast travel and movement as well as non-descript “save system improvements” which could mean multiple files, though that doesn’t exactly help me now. The DLC itself will also add new customization options to the character creator for both players and Pawns, and new Arena dungeons scattered around the existing world will help break up the monotony of revisiting places you’ve already been if you’re replaying it. So Capcom’s clearly trying. The best thing Capcom could possibly do is make the DLC accessible very, very early in the game if you know where to go and are a high enough level to survive it, and make it super easy to get to either via an oxcart or something else. But for now, without that reassurance, I’m looking at my newly begun New Game+ file sideways and wondering if I should even bother.

Dragon’s Dogma 2‘s Dark Arisen DLC is out on October 9 alongside the launch of the game on Nintendo Switch 2. It’ll be included in the Switch 2 edition, and be a separate paid expansion on all other platforms. Quality-of-life updates arrive on June 10 (tomorrow), and more coming at the end of August look to ease your New Game+ speedrun to the DLC, so keep an eye out for those and get your Arisen ready for a journey to the frozen north, provided it doesn’t require another 60+ hours of game to get there.



https://kotaku.com/dragons-dogma-2-dlc-nintendo-switch-2-direct-2000704568

item.title: Splatoon Raiders – Nintendo Direct: June 2026 trailer and screenshots; Splatoon Raiders Direct set for June 30
item.content:encoded:
• Switch 2

Latest look at the single-player Splatoon adventure.

Nintendo has released a new trailer, screenshots, and information for Splatoon Raiders, its new single-player focused Splatoon adventure, as well as announced that it will host a Splatoon Raiders Direct live stream on June 30.

Here are the details:

Splatoon Raiders

In this new single-player-focused Splatoon adventure, you’ll hunt for treasure on the mysterious Spirhalite Islands alongside Deep Cut, a trio of swashbuckling musicians. Customize your own look and deploy weapons and upgradeable gadgets to suit your play style. Level up and get stronger as you battle Salmonid enemies, with a member of Deep Cut by your side riding in an Exploration Bot during your raids.

Splatoon Raiders is ink-splattered fun for seasoned Inklings and newcomers alike!

Daily Comics

Starting June 23, you can read official daily comics exclusively on the Nintendo Today! smart device app setting up the story leading up to Splatoon Raiders.

Splatoon 3 North American League 2026 Playoffs and Splatfest

On June 20 to June 21, the Splatoon 3 North American League 2026 Playoffs get underway with livestreams beginning 12:00 p.m. PT both days. Plus, in collaboration with this game, a Splatfest themed around Splatoon Raiders is coming to the Splatoon 3 game starting July 10 at 5:00 p.m. PT through July 12 at 5:00 p.m. PT!

Deep Cut-Themed Joy-Con 2 Controller

Keep an eye out for new Joy-Con 2 controller colors inspired by Deep Cut, launching the same day as the game!

Splatoon Raiders is due out for Switch 2 on July 23.

Watch the trailer below. View the screenshots at the gallery.

item.title: Minecraft coming to Switch 2 in 2026
item.content:encoded:
• Switch 2

A native release for Nintendo's more powerful handheld.

Mojang Studios will release a native Switch 2 version of Minecraft in 2026, the developer announced.

Here are the details:

Experience Vibrant Visuals on Nintendo Switch 2

Vibrant Visuals is Minecraft‘s biggest visual graphics update to date, and it’ll come enabled as the default graphics setting in the native Switch 2 version!

Released in June 2025 alongside our “Chase the Skies” update, Vibrant Visuals transforms the way players experience Minecraft—with improved visual elements such as directional lighting, volumetric fog, and more. The result is dazzling—shadows move as the sun arcs across the sky, sunshine reflects in the water, light plays on the bottoms of lakes, and clouds appear infinite as they stretch far into the distance.

Super Mario Mash-Up is transformed with Vibrant Visuals

To celebrate the launch of the native version of Minecraft for the Nintendo Switch 2, there will also be an update to the much-loved Super Mario Mash-Up pack using enhanced Vibrant Visuals graphics!

The Super Mario Mash-Up pack is exclusive to Nintendo versions of Minecraft, and it brings the world of Mario into Minecraft with themed textures, music and skins—alongside iconic locations to explore, all inspired by the Super Mario Bros. series! Venture to Peach’s castle, explore Delfino Plaza and experience first-hand how this exciting world comes to life in new ways with the addition of Vibrant Visuals!

Get ready for Minecraft on the Switch 2. Coming soon!

Watch the announcement trailer below.

Switch 2 Announce Trailer

item.title: Pikuniku 2 announced for Switch 2, PC
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • Switch 2

Puzzle adventure game sequel due out in 2027.

Publisher Devolver Digital and developer Sectordub have announced puzzle adventure game sequel Pikuniku 2 for Switch 2 and PC (Steam). It will launch in 2027.

Here is an overview of the game, via Devolver Digital:

About

Piku and his friends pack a picnic and set off once more to whimsically wander through the wonderfully weird. Our story begins with an escaped sandwich and a boat wreck, and only gets odder from there. With the friends scattered across the archipelago, Piku sets off to find them and ultimately return home.

But as with all great adventures, it’s the journey that counts rather than the destination. Along the way Piku will meet many odd folk, each with a story to tell. Their tall tales include whisperings of giant fruit, malevolent robot frogs, and an all-powerful potato.

  • ❮
  • ❯

Key Features

  • Deep State Conspiracies – To the casual observer, Piku’s world brims with delight, but the vivid exterior hides a sinister plot to squeeze the people for every drop of profit. Join the resistance.
  • Playful Puzzles – Use all of Piku’s surprising abilities to solve increasingly weird puzzles along the way. The challenge is never greater than the fun you’ll have solving them.
  • Strange Encounters – No one seems to quite know what they’re doing, least of all Piku. The sense of gentle bafflement permeates every encounter, but piecing them together brings ultimate understanding.
  • Eye-Melting Environments – Primary colors hold no fear for Piku. This is a vibrant world of bold contrasts, brazenly rendered in full 3D, multiplying the possibilities for navigation, exploration, and puzzle-ation.

Watch the announcement trailer below. View the first screenshots at the gallery.

Announce Trailer

item.title: RuneScape: Dragonwilds adds Switch 2 version
item.content:encoded:
• Switch 2

Due out alongside other versions on September 15.

Open-world survival crafting game RuneScape: Dragonwilds will launch for Switch 2 alongside PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam and Epic Games Store when it leaves Early Access on September 15, the company announced.

RuneScape: Dragonwilds first launched in Early Access for PC via Steam on April 15, 2025.

“RuneScape: Dragonwilds was built around player freedom allowing them to explore, grind skills, craft, and create unforgettable adventures together,” said RuneScape: Dragonwilds executive producer Jesse America in a press release. “Nintendo Switch 2 gives players a whole new way to experience the continent of Ashenfall, whether they’re jumping into [cooperative] sessions with friends or continuing their journey on the move. We’re excited to bring the full Dragonwilds experience to Nintendo players this September.”

Here is an overview of the game, via Jagex:

About

et them through battles, but find their practical uses as well, as they collect items and craft things to help them throughout their journey. In this land riddled with fierce dragons, players must level up skills, prepare potions, and craft gear, as they carve a path through these ferocious beasts and make their way to the Dragon Queen herself.

Key Features

  • RuneScape at Its Core! – Embark on quests, level skills while exploring a beautiful, hand-crafted world. This all-new adventure blends high-fantasy and iconic RuneScape lore on an undiscovered continent. Battle legendary RuneScape creatures like Zogres, challenge Black Knights and the entirely new and fearsome Garou, all set to a sweeping soundtrack of reorchestrated RuneScape classics and hours of brand-new music.
  • Grind Skills, Slay Dragons – Locate them, throw down the gauntlet, and carve a path to the Dragon Queen herself. Follow the trail of destruction and uncover the mysteries of the continent to learn not just how to confront the dragons, but how to overcome them.
  • Survival Through Sorcery – Summon a spectral axe to fell trees in an instant! Blink away from danger in a cloud of magic. Turn bones into… peaches? The Anima-rich landscape of Ashenfall offers a power unlike any other. Conjure a tornado of fish from a lake! Explode ore veins with a snap of your fingers! Journey through the vibrant regions of Ashenfall, discover why wild magic spills from the fractured lands and harness that power to survive!
  • Community Driven, Player Centric – Like all RuneScape games, community is at the heart of RuneScape: Dragonwilds. Whether players are adventuring with friends or going it alone, every opinion matters, and it’s up to the community to help shape the future of our game.

Watch a new trailer below.

Switch 2 Announce Trailer

item.title: Whoops! Sand: Raiders of Sophie's server slam got so slammed it isn't coming out tomorrow anymore
item.content:encoded:
Welp! Just yesterday, Sand: Raiders of Sophie devs Hologryph and TowerHaus shared the release times of the desert-set extraction shooter where you have your own steampunk mobile fortress, which was set for release tomorrow, June 10th. Today, however? That release date has gone out the window.

As detailed in a new Steam blog post, this is due to the recently held server slam held over the weekend which apparently did not go over all that well. "It’s been a crazy few days, with Sand featuring at Summer Games Fest and the Server Slam exceeding our expectations on the number of players," the post explains. "The community has also been extremely vocal and critical… It’s clear we need to more closely involve the community in our development, and so we’ve decided to release into Early Access to work towards the best possible version of Sand: Raiders of Sophie."

Previously the game was intended for a full release as opposed to early access, for some context. With that change of release format comes a new release date too, at least: June 22nd, just under two weeks away, so that wait isn't all that much longer. The big issue the devs plan to focus on is to improve the game's server capacity, as how it currently is won't be strong enough for launch. Another server slam is now planned for June 15th for further testing and to "make sure things are smooth."

Once the game does launch, assuming that second test goes well, a roadmap is planned to be shared as well. Certainly a bit of a mess considering their big appearance at Geoff Fest, one of the biggest shows of the year, but I suppose that's video games in 2026!



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/whoops-sand-raiders-of-sophies-server-slam-got-so-slammed-it-isnt-coming-out-tomorrow-anymore
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Look, You Can’t All Avoid GTA 6 By Hiding In September And October
item.content:encoded:
Summer Game Fest season is officially over now that Nintendo has dropped its latest Direct. And uh, folks, I don’t know if you know this, but there are way, way too many video games launching in the same relatively short span of time. All of them are seemingly doing this to avoid Grand Theft Auto 6 in November. But I really, really hope some of them reconsider.

When the Nintendo Direct wrapped up earlier today with a tiny teaser for the Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time remake coming later this year, I hopped over to my list of games launching between the final days of August and the first days of November and started updating it. It was already a big list thanks to Sony and Xbox and others all trying to avoid GTA 6, which arrives on November 19. But following the last few days of announcements and this morning’s Direct, this list has become hard to look at without wincing in pain. Let’s take a look, shall we:

  • Resonance: A Plague Tale Legacy – August 27
  • Star Wars Zero Company – August 27
  • Elden Ring Tarnished Edition (Switch 2) – August 28
  • The Blood of Dawnwalker – September 3
  • Orbitals – September 3
  • Halloween – September 8
  • Screenbound – September 10
  • Wolverine – September 15
  • Trine 6 – September 17
  • Warhammer 40K: Dawn of War IV – September 17
  • Trails in the Sky 2nd Chapter – September 17
  • Fire Emblem: Fortune’s Weave – September 17
  • Lego Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight (Switch 2) – September 18
  • Dune Awakening (console release) – September 22
  • Valor Mortis – September 24
  • Hot Wheels Infinite Rush – September 24
  • Control Resonant – September 24
  • Silent Hill: Townfall – September 24
  • Onimusha: Way of the Sword – September 25
  • Ace Combat 8: Wings of Theve – September 29 (Early Access)/ Oct. 2
  • Minecraft Dungeons 2 – September 29
  • Rayman Legends Retold – October 1
  • Dynasty Warriors 3 Remastered – October 1
  • Gears of War: E-Day – October 6
  • Star Wars: Galactic Racer – October 6
  • Kingdom Hearts 1-3 Switch 2 ports – October 8
  • Dragon’s Dogma 2: Dark Arisen – October 9
  • Planet Zoo 2 – October 13
  • Castlevania: Belmont’s Curse – October 13
  • Toy Story Retro Round Up / Toy Story 3 Remaster – October 13
  • Tales of Eternia Remastered – October 16
  • Final Fantasy Resonance – October 22
  • Nintendo Switch Sports Resort – October 22
  • One Piece Grand Gourmet – October 23
  • CoD: MW4 – October 23
  • Hello Kitty Party Land – October 29
  • Phantom Blade Zero – October 29
  • No Rest for the Wicked – October
  • Godzilla Destroy All Monsters – November 3

A few things to keep in mind: There are some Switch 2 ports I didn’t include because not all of them are super high-priority, and this list was already so big. Also, it’s very possible I missed something that should be on here. Please let me know in the comments.

That said, the list above is already massive and filled with just about every kind of game you can imagine, from big AAA titles to indie games, to sequels, to remakes, remasters, and retro re-releases. There’s something for everyone. In fact, there’s too much. Far too much. Some of these need to be delayed until 2027. Please.

Some good reasons to delay your game!

Sure, you can argue that you don’t have to play a game on release day. Which is true! But so much of the industry is built around those first few days and weeks when a cool new game (ideally) has its time in the spotlight, perhaps dominating the cultural conversation or at least being part of it. Studios can get shut down if a game flops out of the gate, and that can’t be undone if a game finds its audience later on.

When so many things launch in a short span of time, stuff gets buried quickly by the algorithm and by other games, making it harder for people to even find out that a game they might want to buy has already launched. It’s a bad situation for gamers and devs.

And of course, for us in games media, this list is terrifying. Even if all the sites covering this field that used to exist still did and they were staffed properly, this would be a scary list. But the games media in 2026 isn’t close to being prepared to properly take on the tidal wave of games, big and small, about to hit us in September and October. And that sucks for readers, game makers, publishers, fans, and everyone else involved.

So I’m here to ask that at least, like…20 of these games get pushed back until February or March of 2027. Or maybe one or two can be brave and launch around GTA 6‘s release date. Sure, you’re taking on GTA 6, the biggest video game release in decades. But time it right and you’ll have a full week or more mostly to yourself before Rockstar’s giant open-world game arrives and buries everything.

Anyway, good luck out there, folks. Pace yourselves, wait for sales, and drink plenty of water.



https://kotaku.com/look-you-cant-all-avoid-gta-6-by-hiding-in-october-and-september-2000703984

item.title: Earth Defense Force 5 coming to Switch 2 on October 8; Earth Defense Force 6 this winter
item.content:encoded:
• Switch 2

The latest entries in the alien 3D action shooter series.

D3 Publisher and developer Sandlot will release a Switch 2 version of Earth Defense Force 5 on October 8 worldwide, followed by Earth Defense Force 6 this winter, the companies announced. Both will support English, Japanese, Korean, Traditional Chinese, and Simplified Chinese language options. The Switch 2 versions are ported by Yuke’s.

Earth Defense Force 5 is currently available for PlayStation 4 and PC via Steam, while Earth Defense Force 6 is available for PlayStation 5, PlayStation 4, and PC via Steam and Epic Games Store.

Here is an overview of each game, via D3 Publisher:

Earth Defense Force 5, the action shooter enjoyed by 2 million users worldwide on PlayStation®4, PlayStation®5 and PC, is coming to Nintendo Switch 2. Players will be able to experience the overwhelming tension of colossal enemies closing in, along with the exhilarating feeling of taking down massive waves of foes.

The Earth Defense Force series began in 2003 as one title in the SIMPLE2000 Series. Earth Defense Force 5 refreshes the world setting and begins a new story, making it accessible for new recruits as well as long-time Earth Defense Force soldiers.

Earth Defense Force 6, the complete sequel to Earth Defense Force 5 and the latest numbered entry in the series, is also planned for release on Nintendo Switch 2 this winter. Further details will be announced at a later date.

item.title: Lords of the Fallen II adds Switch 2 version
item.content:encoded:
• Switch 2

Due out this fall.

Soulslike action RPG Lords of the Fallen II will launch for Switch 2 alongside its previously announced PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC (Steam, Epic Games Store) versions this fall, publisher CI Games and developer Hexworks announced.

“Our team is hard at work to ensure Lords of the Fallen II‘s signature dark fantasy world and fast, brutal combat translate directly to the Nintendo Switch 2, and we’re very happy with how the game currently looks and plays,” said game director James Lowe in a press release. “This marks the first time that Nintendo players will explore the realms of Axiom and Umbral, and these new Lampbearers deserve to experience the game at the best quality possible on a great platform. Thanks to the team’s work and the support from Nintendo and Epic, we’re confident it will be a fantastic way to play Lords of the Fallen II.”

  • ❮
  • ❯

Here is an overview of the game, via CI Games:

Lords of the Fallen II on Nintendo Switch 2 delivers impressive Unreal Engine 5 visuals in both hand-held and docked modes, targeting a stable 30 frames per second. Using both Nanite and Lumen technology, the game achieves higher poly models and fully dynamic global illumination alongside other bespoke optimizations, thanks to careful engineering coordination between the development team, Epic and Nintendo.

CI Games is aiming to deliver a “bolder, braver, and bloodier” evolution of its flagship franchise with Lords of the Fallen II, building directly upon extensive community feedback from the 2023 title and the lessons learned through more than 70 post-launch updates. This sequel pushes the series further into its signature aggressive combat style with an improved combat system designed to feel faster, more responsive, and more satisfying than ever before, with brutal executions, expanded combat abilities and visceral dismemberments. Lords of the Fallen II will also feature a richer and more varied world, a significantly expanded enemy roster with biome-specific factions, more dynamic boss encounters, shared-progression co-op at launch and a more immersive Umbral realm.

Watch the announcement trailer below. View the screenshots at the gallery.

Switch 2 Announce Trailer

item.title: Dragonball Z Board Game Kickstarter Has Gone Live, Breaking Past Its Limits
item.content:encoded:
The Dragonball Z Board Game Kickstarter has gone live. Pop Art Games and Lynnvander Games' tabletop adaptation of one of the most iconic Shonen series in the world has gone live on the platform, reaching its initial funding goal in just a few minutes.

The Dragonball Z Board Game Kickstarter Campaign

According to the campaign page, the Dragonball Z Board Game is a cooperative tabletop experience created by longtime fans of the franchise. The game's campaign, designed for 1-4 players, can span over 15 hours of play time. The campaign is broken up into 12 "Episodes," adapting major story arcs from the Dragonball Z series, starting at Raditz' arrival on Earth and ending with the battle against Majin Buu on the Planet of the Kais.

Throughout these episodes, players will choose from one of several characters from the series. From there, players can recruit allies, travel to iconic locations from the series, and get special items. Each episode's relevant materials are in several envelopes, allowing you to store and suspend your game whenever you are done playing.

Since this is Dragonball Z, your characters will also train, increase their Power Level, manage Stress and Energy, and unlock new abilities as the campaign progresses, letting you relive or re-imagine key points from the series. The game even has a mechanic where you can gather the seven Dragon Balls to make powerful wishes or to bring fallen allies back to life.

Alternatively, you can play each episode as a standalone experience or enjoy the game's Freeform mode. Freeform allows you to mix and match fighters, enemies, and locations into your own custom episodes. 

Notably, the Dragonball Z Board Game Kickstarter is a sort of wished revival of its own. The game was originally slated to come out under IDW Games before the company went under and all orders of the game were cancelled in 2021. This Kickstarter is for that same board game with the same designers at the helm.

There are only two pledge levels for the campaign. One is the Standard Edition of the game at an MSRP of $95. This includes everything you need to play the game. For those with deeper pockets, there is the Premium Edition at $119. This version of the game includes premium card dividers and higher quality dice.

At the time of writing, the Dragonball Z Board Game Kickstarter has raised $69,982, almost eight times its initial funding goal of $9.000. The campaign ends on July 7, 2026.

Ever since he was small, Tyler Chancey has had a deep, abiding love for video games and a tendency to think and overanalyze everything he enjoyed. This… More about Tyler



https://techraptor.net/tabletop/news/dragonball-z-board-game-kickstarter-has-gone-live-breaking-past-its-limits

item.title: LoL Patch 26.12 Champion Buffs and Nerfs
item.content:encoded:
League of Legends (LoL) Patch 26.12 has had its patch notes revealed, with a lot of champion buffs and nerfs.

As the penultimate patch before the MSI tournament, Riot have made big changes to try and add diversity to the current League of Legends meta.

And the game will get an even bigger shift when new League of Legends champion Locke releases on 26.13.

But for now, here are all the major changes coming in LoL Patch 26.12, including AP Xin Zhao nerfs and a huge update to Teleport.

Apex Duo Restrictions

Apex duo restrictions were turned on with the start of Season 2 in select regions, where Apex players also received a ranked reset.

Riot is monitoring the health of top-tier matches in those regions to understand what improvements need to be made. As a result, Apex duo queue will remain unavailable for now.

LoL Patch 26.12 Champion Buffs

Aatrox

While Aaotrox remains solid pick in the current meta, Riot thinks he needs a boost, so is putting more power into his Q sweet spot to reward his key skill check and make him more threatening.

  • Q – The Darkin Blade
    • Sweet Spot Bonus Damage: 70 ⇒ 75

Gwen

Gwen lost some power with 26.09’s minor rework to Dusk and Dawn, so Riot is buffing her early game. She’ll remain a scaling pick, but a weaker first item spike has cost her early game a bit too much.

  • Q – Snip Snip!
    • Base Damage Per Snip: 10/13.5/17/20.5/24 ⇒ 10/14/18/22/26
  • E -Skip ‘n Slash
    • Bonus Attack Speed: 20/35/50/65/80% ⇒ 30/42.5/55/67.5/80%

Hwei

Hwei’s 26.08 changes to how his ability-slows stack and interact with other sources of slows had a larger negative impact than expected.

The last compensation buff wasn’t enough, so Riot is trying to ensure his Q and E abilities remain relevant.

  • Q – Subject: Disaster
    • QQ Damage: 50 / 80 / 110 / 140 / 170 (+70% AP) (+3 / 4 / 5 / 6 / 7% Target Max HP) ⇒ 50 / 80 / 110 / 140 / 170 (+80% AP) (+3 / 4 / 5 / 6 / 7% Target Max HP)
    • QW Damage: 60 / 85 / 110 / 135 / 160 (+25% AP), increased to up to 120 / 201.875 / 302.5 / 421.875 / 560 (+50 / 59.375 / 68.75 / 78.125 / 87.5% AP) ⇒ 60 / 85 / 110 / 135 / 160 (+30% AP), increased to up to 120 / 201.875 / 302.5 / 421.875 / 560 (+60 / 71.25 / 82.5 / 93.75 / 105% AP)
    • QE Slow: 30% ⇒ 35%
  • E – Subject: Torment
    • Cooldown: 13 / 12.5 / 12 / 11.5 / 11s ⇒ 12 / 11.5 / 11 / 10.5 / 10s

Jax

Riot want Jax to perform better against more durable opponents without overbuffing him into OP territory.

  • Q – Leap Strike
    • Mana Cost: 65 ⇒ 50
  • E – Counter Strike
    • Target Max Health Damage: 3.5% minimum damage / 7% maximum damage ⇒ 4% minimum damage / 8% maximum damage

Sylas

Sylas has come out worse for wear since the start of Season 2.

Riot believes he is still a powerful early lane bully, but his scaling potential is currently much too low.

  • Q – Chain Lash
    • Initial Damage: 40 / 60 / 80 / 100 / 120 (+40% AP) ⇒ 40 / 65 / 90 / 115 / 140 (+45% AP)
  • W – Kingslayer
    • Heal Amount: 20 / 40 / 60 / 80 / 100 (+20% AP) (+5% Sylas’ Bonus HP), increased by up to 100% based on Sylas’ missing health ⇒ 20 / 40 / 60 / 80 / 100 (+30% AP) (+5% Sylas’ Bonus HP), increased by up to 100% based on Sylas’ missing health

Syndra

Riot is hoping that buffing Syndra’s hard-to-hit Q will give players enough reason to start playing her again after her more reliable damage was stripped from her W and R.

  • Base Stats
    • Base Health: 563 ⇒ 583
  • Q – Dark Sphere
    • Damage: 80-220 (+65% AP) ⇒ 90-230 (+70% AP)

Tristana

Riot concedes it doesn’t want marksem to dominate the mid lane meta again, but think there’s still room for some.

Riot is trying to help Tristana become viable mid again by stripping back the mana cost nerfs to her Q from a couple of seasons back and helping her level scaling.

  • Base Stats
    • AD Growth: 2.9 ⇒ 3.4
  • Q – Rapid Fire
    • Mana: 30 / 35 / 40 / 45 / 50 ⇒ 15 / 20 / 25 / 30 / 35

Yuumi

As the only champion in the game with built-in Heal/Shield power, Yuumi was hit hardest by the Moonstone Renewer mechanics changes in 26.09.

Riot is giving her AP ratios a compensation buff to help keep enchanter items optimal for her.

  • Passive – Feline Friendship
    • Heal Amount: 20 – 110 (+25% AP) (Levels 1 – 18) ⇒ 20 – 110 (+30% AP) (Levels 1 – 18)
  • E – Zoomies
    • Shield Amount: 65 / 90 / 115 / 140 / 165 (+30% AP) ⇒ 65 / 90 / 115 / 140 / 165 (+40% AP)

LoL Patch 26.12 Champion Nerfs

Lee Sin

The recent changes taking away some of Lee Sin’s kit restrictions have been exciting for players, but he’s become too powerful now.

With his increased outplay potential, Riot is nerfing his raw damage to balance him out.

  • Base Stats
    • AD Growth: 3.7 ⇒ 3.4
  • Q1 – Sonic Wave
    • Damage: 65 / 95 / 125 / 155 / 185 (+95% bonus AD) ⇒ 60 / 90 / 120 / 150 / 180 (+90% bonus AD)
  • Q2 – Resonating Strike
    • Damage: 65 / 95 / 125 / 155 / 185 (+95% bonus AD) ⇒ 60 / 90 / 120 / 150 / 180 (+90% bonus AD)

Nocturne

Nocturne has been in a strong spot for a while, especially in pro play.

Riot likes how distinct he feels to play with his relatively low ultimate cooldown, so are going to pull power out of his 1v1 and farming since he has such strong tools to quickly create unequal fights.

  • Q – Duskbringer
    • Damage: 65 / 110 / 155 / 200 / 245 ⇒ 65 / 105 / 145 / 185 / 225

Orianna

As the highest pro priority in recent patches, Riot feels like too much of Orianna’s power is in early lane dominance. They still want her to be strong, but to earn success with great Shockwaves rather than lane bullying.

  • Base Stats
    • Base Health: 585 ⇒ 565
  • Passive – Clockwork Windup
    • Damage Increase Per Stack: 20% ⇒ 15%
  • R – Command: Shockwave
    • Damage: 250/400/550 (+95% AP) ⇒ 225/350/475 (+110% AP)

Ryze

Ryze’s early game trading has only gotten stronger with Deathfire Touch.

For a champion known as a scaling combo mage, Riot feel his laning has become too difficult to deal with.

So, similarly to Orianna, Riot is tuning down his early game a bit to keep his power budget in the late game as he’s intended to be.

  • Base Stats
    • Base Health: 645 ⇒ 620
    • Base AD: 58 ⇒ 55

Varus

Varus has become a huge priority pick in both top and bot in pro play.

While the changes to Experimental Hexplate should help alleviate his dominance in top lane, in bot lane, Riot is looking to further disincentivize his oppressive early game-focused lethality builds, while being relatively less impactful to the most common on-hit builds and helping them scale a bit better.

  • Passive – Living Vengeance
    • On Non-Champ Kill AD and AP: 10% of his Total AS ⇒ 11% of his Total AS
    • On Champ Kill AD and AP: 25% of his Total AS ⇒ 33% of his Total AS
    • On Non-Champ Kill AS: +50% AS ⇒ +30% AS
  • Q – Piercing Arrow
    • Damage: 53.33 / 100 / 146.67 / 193.33 / 240 (+100% bonus AD) to 80 / 150 / 220 / 290 / 360 (+150% bonus AD) ⇒ 53.33 / 100 / 146.67 / 193.33 / 240 (+80% bonus AD) to 80 / 150 / 220 / 290 / 360 (+120% bonus AD)
  • W – Blighted Quiver
    • On-Hit Damage: 8 / 17 / 26 / 35 / 44 (+15% bonus AD) (+25% AP) ⇒ 4 / 13 / 22 / 31 / 40 (+15% bonus AD) (+25% AP)

Xin Zhao

AP Xin Zhao continues being far too strong in mid lane. So, Riot is following up last patch’s adjustment with a straight nerf this time to his ability to sustain both his health and mana in lane.

  • Passive – Determination
    • Healing: 3 / 4 / 5% Max HP (+45 / 55 / 80% AP) ⇒ 2 / 3.5 / 5% Max HP (+40 / 50 / 70% AP)
  • W – Wind Becomes Lightning
    • Mana Cost: 60 / 55 / 50 / 45 / 40 ⇒ 60
  • E – Audacious Charge
    • Mana Cost: 50 ⇒ 60

Teleport Ajustment

In another huge change for the game, Riot is updating the Teleport Summoner Spell a players were using it for the shield in fights instead of cross-map plays

  • Max Health Shield
    • 30% for 30 seconds ⇒ 35% for 10 seconds

What do you think of all these changes? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

Meanwhile, even bigger changes are coming next year as the League Next update will overhaul the support role and other parts of the game.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/lol-patch-26-12-champion-buffs-and-nerfs/

item.title: Xenoblade Chronicles series Nintendo Switch 2 Editions announced
item.content:encoded:
• Switch 2

Xenoblade Chronicles: Definitive Edition - Nintendo Switch 2 Edition out now.

Publisher Nintendo and developer Monolith Soft have released Xenoblade Chronicles: Definitive Edition – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition via Nintendo eShop for $69.99. Users who own the Switch version can purchase an “Upgrade Pack” for $9.99. A physical edition will launch on July 30 for $69.99.

Publisher Nintendo and developer Monolith Soft have announced Nintendo Switch 2 Edition versions of the Xenoblade Chronicles games, starting with Xenoblade Chronicles: Definitive Edition – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition, which is available now via Nintendo eShop.

The release schedule for each game is as follows:

  • Xenoblade Chronicles: Definitive Edition – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition
    • Digital: June 9, 2026
    • Physical: July 30, 2026
  • Xenoblade Chronicles 2 – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition
    • Digital: July 30, 2026
    • Physical: October 1, 2026
  • Xenoblade Chronicles 3 – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition
    • Digital: December 3, 2026
    • Physical: December 3, 2026

Here is an overview of each game, via Nintendo:

Xenoblade Chronicles: Definitive Edition – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition

  • ❮
  • ❯

About

Join the fight between man and machine in this critically acclaimed RPG—now enhanced for the Nintendo Switch 2 system!
Discover the origins of Shulk as he and his companions clash against a seemingly unstoppable mechanical menace. Wield a future-seeing blade, chain together attacks, and carefully position your party members in strategic, real-time combat. Journey across a massive world that has been enhanced to run at 4K* resolution in TV mode, and full HD in handheld mode, at 60 frames per second—with smoother and sharper cutscenes.

This Nintendo Switch 2 Edition game contains all the additions and improvements of Xenoblade Chronicles: Definitive Edition on the Nintendo Switch system, including the extended story of Xenoblade Chronicles: Future Connected (which has also been enhanced), remixed and remastered music tracks, better quest tracking, and more. It also includes some additional content for you to enjoy!

Race Across the Bionis and Mechonis on a High-powered Vehicle

Introducing the Ether Jet: a vehicle that will greatly enhance the speed at which you can traverse the Bionis and Mechonis, both in the main game and in the Future Connected story. But this is not just a convenience feature—you can also race competitively against the clock or your party members at racetracks throughout the world and earn in-game rewards in the process.

Unlock Additional Equipment, Experience Fully Voiced Heart-to-Heart Conversations, and More

Get equipment made for riding the Ether Jet, and experience improved Heart-to-Heart scenes with fully voiced dialogue for the first time!

Nintendo Switch 2 Features

  • Experience Shulk’s battle against a seemingly insurmountable mechanical menace in 4K resolution in TV mode, and full HD in handheld mode, at 60 frames per second.
  • Traverse the Bionis and Mechonis, two titans frozen in battle, at high speed on the Ether Jet. Get additional equipment made specifically for riding the Ether Jet.
  • The Xenoblade Chronicles: Future Connected story has also been enhanced with visual improvements and the Ether Jet.
  • Experience fully voiced Heart-to-Heart conversations for the first time.

View a set of screenshots at the gallery.

Xenoblade Chronicles 2 – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition

  • ❮
  • ❯

About

Lead Rex, Pyra, and the world to refuge in an adventure enhanced for the Nintendo Switch 2 system!

Lost in a sea of clouds, entire civilizations rest on the backs of Titans. As the giant beasts march toward death, the last hope is a scavenger named Rex—and Pyra, a living weapon known as a Blade. Command a group of Blades and lead them to countless strategic victories as you search for the fabled paradise Pyra calls home. This journey has been enhanced with 4K* resolution in TV mode, and full HD in handheld mode, at 60 frames per second—and with smoother and sharper cutscenes.

Discover a new rare Blade—and control your Blades to fight in mercenary squad battles

Not only is there an additional Blade out there for you to find—along with a quest to complete—but you can directly command your Blades in a new action battle mode!

Unlock new equipment designs for Pyra and Mythra

Customize the look of Pyra and Mythra with new equipment designs exclusive to this Nintendo Switch 2 Edition game.

Nintendo Switch 2 Features

  • Experience a journey across Titans in 4K resolution in TV mode, and full HD in handheld mode, at 60 frames per second.
  • Discover a new rare Blade, complete with a quest for you to take on. Directly control the Blades you send on mercenary missions in a new action battle mode.
  • Unlock new equipment designs for Pyra and Mythra.

View a set of screenshots at the gallery.

Xenoblade Chronicles 3 – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition

  • ❮
  • ❯

About

Live to fight. Fight to live. Now with enhancements for the Nintendo Switch 2 system.

Join Noah and Mio, members of the two opposing nations of Keves and Agnus, on a heartfelt journey through a warring world with a dark secret. Traverse massive, fantastical landscapes and master seamless real-time RPG combat that has all been enhanced to run at 4K* resolution in TV mode, and full HD in handheld mode, at 60 frames per second with smoother and sharper cutscenes.

This Nintendo Switch 2 Edition also includes some additional content! Recruit a new Hero—complete with an additional quest—and put your strength to the test against waves of enemies in a new defensive battle mode. The game also features newly voiced event scenes.

Nintendo Switch 2 Features

  • Traverse massive, fantastical landscapes in 4K resolution in TV mode, and full HD in handheld mode, at 60 frames per second.
  • Recruit a new Hero, complete with a quest for you to take on.
  • Put your strength to the test in defensive battles against waves of enemies.
  • Experience fully voiced event scenes.

View a set of screenshots at the gallery.

Watch a set of announcement trailers below.

Series Announce Trailer

English

Japanese

Xenoblade Chronicles: Definitive Edition – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition Overview Trailer

English

Japanese

item.title: Sand: Raiders of Sophia Delayed Day Before Launch, Now Will Be Early Access
item.content:encoded:
Sand: Raiders of Sophia, the build your own giant fortress mech that travels about the desert battling foes, has received a delay only a day before it was set to be released.

It's not like the Sand: Raiders of Sophia release date was a longstanding affair either - instead it had been announced on Friday, at Geoff Keighley's Summer Game Fest, with a server slam available right then for people to try. The original June 10th release date was still being shared as of yesterday on Steam, even as the server slam revealed that there were cascading issues that happened when servers crossed 4k users per region.

What is the new Sand: Raiders of Sophia release date?

According to their new announcement, the new release date for Sand: Raiders of Sophia is June 22nd. They are examining all their server slam logs and attempting to fix a variety of bugs and structural issues that showed up form the issues, before hosting another Sand: Raiders of Sophia server slam on June 15th, which hopefully won't reveal more cascading structural issues or bugs.

Why is Sand: Raiders of Sophia now going to Early Access?

Recognizing they may not be able to handle a huge influx of players in only a couple of weeks, the developers are labeling themselves as Early Access so they have somewhat lower populations to work with. Additionally, they state it's "clear we need to more closely involve the community in our development", with a lot of feedback and critique in various forms provided to them. By going in early access they hope that will help them work towards the best version of Sand: Raiders of Sophia. 

Additionally on the new launch date of June 22nd, the developer plans to provide a roadmap based on feedback and suggestions from the community. This roadmap will be iterated on and updated throughout early access.

A longtime lover of speculative fiction, in almost all its forms, Don joined TechRaptor in 2014 on a whim sending in an application as he was looking for… More about Don
